tags.name: Paradies Corner
tags.qa_notes: Limited site-specific sourcing; lettuce coral, Napoleon wrasse, hawksbill turtles confirmed across 3 sources. Detailed depth/current profiles not found in published sources.

tags.name: Blue Corner
tags.qa_notes: Well-documented site. 13+ shark species recorded. Grey reef sharks primary species confirmed by 3+ sources. Reef hook origin confirmed.

tags.name: Chandelier Cave
tags.qa_notes: Depth corrected from 15m to 11m (36ft per Fish N Fins). 5-chamber structure confirmed. Entrance opening diameter 10ft/3m at 12ft/4m depth confirmed across sources.

tags.description: WWII Japanese coastal freighter. Bombed Operation Desecrate One March 30-31 1944; sank evening April 1 1944. Length reported as 285ft (87m) by Fish N Fins or 295ft (90m) by Wrecked In My rEvo — sources conflict. Tonnage reported 1,941 tons (Fish N Fins) or ~2,100 tons (Palau Dive Adventures) — sources conflict. Build year reported 1942 or 1943 — sources conflict. Deck at 30m, keel at 40m, forward kingpost tower at 12m. Coal-burning quadruple-expansion steam engine. Nicknamed Lionfish Wreck. Rediscovered April 1989 by Francis Toribiong and Klaus Lindemann.
tags.name: Chuyo Maru
tags.qa_notes: Wreck deep research conducted. Dimension/tonnage/build-year conflicts flagged across 3 sources — not resolved. Sinking date April 1 1944 confirmed by 3 sources. Discovery 1989 by Toribiong/Lindemann confirmed by 3 sources. Lionfish Wreck nickname confirmed. Live ammunition warning added.

tags.name: Lighthouse Channel
tags.qa_notes: Mandarin fish confirmed by 3 sources as key attraction. Specific critter species claims (seahorses, ghost pipefish, Spanish dancers) not confirmed by 3 independent sources at this specific channel — removed from description, retained as regional notes only.

tags.name: New Drop-Off
tags.qa_notes: Two-wall corner configuration confirmed (mini Blue Corner). Location west of Ngemelis/German Channel confirmed. Gorgonian fans, grey reef sharks, Napoleon wrasse, turtles confirmed across 3 sources.

tags.name: Sand Bar
tags.qa_notes: Depth updated to 22m (cleaning station 18-22m per 3 sources, previously listed as 25m). German Channel history confirmed: blasted 1908-1911 for phosphate transport. Manta species (reef vs oceanic) not confirmed by 3 independent site-specific sources — removed species specifics. Best season Nov-May confirmed.

tags.name: Siaes Corner
tags.qa_notes: Wall and corner configuration confirmed. Grey reef sharks primary species. Lower traffic than Blue Corner confirmed as distinguishing feature.

tags.name: Siaes Tunnel
tags.qa_notes: CRITICAL CORRECTION: Depth corrected from 12m to 40m (tunnel floor). Entrance at ~35m (100ft), floor at ~40m (120ft). Tunnel length 90m confirmed by Fish N Fins. Two natural windows confirmed. Deep diving specialty strongly recommended. Previous 12m depth was severely inaccurate and safety-relevant.

tags.name: Ulong Channel
tags.qa_notes: Lettuce coral formations 15-20ft high confirmed. Garden eel colonies confirmed. Grey and whitetip reef sharks confirmed. Grouper/snapper spawning aggregations April-July full moons confirmed. Current can reverse mid-dive confirmed.

tags.name: Blue Holes
tags.qa_notes: 4 holes confirmed at 1-2m depth. Cavern floor at 40m confirmed. Temple of Doom entrance at ~27m confirmed as requiring full cave certification — multiple fatalities per Fish N Fins. Light effects confirmed. Distance from Koror ~30 miles/50-70 min confirmed.

tags.name: Peliliu Corner
tags.qa_notes: Extreme down-currents documented by operators as primary hazard. Grey reef sharks and silvertip sharks confirmed by 3 sources. Rare megafauna (hammerheads, bull sharks, orcas) noted by operators as occasional. Philippine Sea/Pacific Ocean current convergence confirmed. 90-120 min boat ride from Koror confirmed.

tags.name: The Big Drop Off
tags.qa_notes: Soft corals (violet to pink), leather corals, sea fans confirmed. Pyramid butterflyfish, square anthias, Moorish idols, yellowtail fusiliers confirmed. WWII ball and chain at ~35ft near eastern mooring confirmed. Nurse sharks mentioned by one source (palaudiveadventures.com), not confirmed by 2+ sources — noted with caution.

tags.name: Ngerechong North Coral Garden
tags.qa_notes: Limited site-specific sources available. Reef type confirmed by OSM data and regional operator context. Specific coral density claims and species lists from previous description not confirmed by 3 independent sources — simplified to confirmed facts.

tags.name: Ngerechong Drop-off
tags.qa_notes: Wall type confirmed. Limited site-specific published sources. Species claims from previous description not confirmed by 3 independent sources at this specific site — description simplified to confirmed facts and regional context.

tags.name: Temple of Doom
tags.qa_notes: Accessed via Blue Holes cavern. Full cave certification required per Fish N Fins. Multiple fatalities documented by Fish N Fins — this warning added to all descriptions. Cave entrance at ~27m (80-85ft) from Blue Holes confirmed.

tags.name: Jake SeaPlane
tags.qa_notes: CORRECTION: Previous description stated aircraft was 'shot down during American air raids' — cause of loss is NOT confirmed. Unbent propellers suggest engine not running at water impact; engine failure during takeoff/landing is the leading theory per multiple sources. 'Shot down' claim removed. Aircraft dimensions confirmed via Wikipedia: 11.3m long, 14.5m wingspan. Tail section separated and lies ~6m away heavily obscured by coral (not intact as previously described). Discovered 1994 by local fisherman confirmed by 3 sources. Live ordnance warning added. High tide recommendation for best visibility confirmed.
